rulings.Case Title: THOMAS NUSSBAUM VS. CAL INC.,A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce The Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 12, 2010, line 1, DEFENDANT CAL INC., and, DAVID ESPARZA Motion to Enforce the Settlement Agreement. Motion to enforce the settlement agreement granted. Defendants have paid the $80,000 settlement amount in full, and have used their best efforts to assist plaintiff in securing and obtaining the $89,500.00 in change orders. The case is dismissed with prejudice per the terms of the agreement entered into by the parties. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS NUSSBAUM VS. CAL INC.,A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Re Notice) Of Motion For Court To Define Terms Of Settlement Agreement; And Other Defts To Submit The Proper Documents To Fbop As The Gsa Contract Holder
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 12, 2010, line 1, PLAINTIFF THOMAS NUSSBAUM (Re Notice) of Motion for Court to Define Terms of Settlement Agreement; and Other Defendants to Submit the Proper Documents To FBOP as The GSA Contract Holder. Motion for court to define the terms of the settlement agreement is denied. Agreement entered into on the record in open court on April 28, 2005. Parties agreed that defendants would use their best efforts to get claims processed. Court's determinations of duties and responsibilities to be made on ex parte basis before the drafting of the settlement agreement (RT 3:28-5:7.) Court retained jurisdiction to enforce settlement agreement, executed in August 2005. Court has done so since this motion and related motion calendared in November 2009. Court finds defendants have reasonably and in good faith performed their duty to use their best efforts to process the claims in question. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: AERUS LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY VS. BINGHAM MCCUTCHEN, LLP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Hearing On Defts' Petition To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 12, 2010, line 7, DEFENDANTS BINGHAM MCCUTCHEN, LLP, FRANK HINMAN, WILLIAM ABRAMS amended Notice Of Hearing On Defendants' Petition To Compel Arbitration. Petition to compel arbitration is granted. Plaintiff's objections to evidence overruled. Written agreement contains valid and binding arbitration clause, and applies to "any dispute or claims." Plaintiff fails to present evidence that the agreement is unconscionable or fraudulently induced. Business and Professions Code section 6148 not applicable due to emergency nature of services required and LLC status of client. The agreement dated April 23, 2007 put plaintiff on notice that it was for a new matter and was for an engagement to represent plaintiff to overturn the partial summary judgment "and any other issues as may arise in the litigtion." =(302/CWW)
